exemplar.title: to what extent has marketing strategy (productandpromotion) helped Nvidia with its revenue growth?

detailedExemplarData.title: to what extent has marketing strategy (productandpromotion) helped Nvidia with its revenue growth?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation and sufficient contextual detail
The research question is clearly stated, focused on Nvidiaâs Product and Promotion contributions to revenue growth, and connected throughout the introduction
A suitable range of relevant secondary sources and data visualizations demonstrate informed selection
Minor language flaws do not obscure meaning and the link between marketing strategy and revenue growth is consistently maintained
weaknesses: The introduction contains some repetition and minor tangents suggesting the overview could be tightened
The research question omits a defined time horizon or benchmark, leaving partial ambiguity
Methodology lacks explicit justification for source selection and alternative approaches are not discussed
The conclusion summarises findings but does not explicitly measure âto what extentâ as posed by the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed this work in the upper band for Focus and Method. The investigation communicates its topic and boundaries with accuracy, formulates a clear research question, and outlines an appropriate methodological approach. Minor shortcomings in scope definition (no time horizon) and explanation of source selection prevent a perfect score, but overall performance justifies a 5 out of 6.

strengths: The student demonstrates a clear grasp of core business management concepts and applies them coherently to Nvidiaâs context
Marketing terminology is used accurately in most cases, showing effective command of subject vocabulary
Source material (revenue figures, market-share statistics, advertising spend) is integrated into the discussion and referenced in visuals
Graphs and tools (BCG, Ansoff) are constructed and linked to the analysis, indicating active use of evidence
weaknesses: In-text citations and bibliographic references are inconsistent, undermining data credibility
There are occasional inaccuracies and imprecise phrasing when distinguishing concepts (e.g., GPUs vs. graphics cards)
Several analytical tools remain applied at a descriptive level without fully tailoring insights to Nvidiaâs specific situation
Financial and graphical data lack detailed source attribution and contextual numbers to support claims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student shows clear and coherent knowledge of marketing concepts and mostly appropriate application of sources, inconsistencies in citation, some inaccuracies, and the generic nature of tool applications limit the work to the upper mid-band. This performance merits 4 out of 6.

strengths: Research is clearly relevant to the question with up-to-date secondary sources and demonstrates breadth across financial and strategic data
The student applies analytical tools (BCG, SWOT, Ansoff) and connects revenue trends to strategic choices, showing clear analysis
The argument progresses logically, weighing Product versus Promotion influence and acknowledging tool limitations
weaknesses: Analysis often remains surface-level and reiterates textbook definitions without probing causal mechanisms or employing quantitative techniques
Critical evaluation is limited, with minimal exploration of alternative explanations, competitor context, or deeper risk assessment
Claims about market share, profitability drivers, and promotional ROI lack triangulation and in-depth supporting evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Critical Thinking fall within the âgoodâ research and clear analysis band but do not reach the sophistication or depth required for higher marks. While the argument is coherent and tool-based analysis is evident, the lack of detailed critical evaluation and depth of insight constrains the score to 4 out of 12.

strengths: The report follows a conventional structure with title page, headings, and embedded visuals
Basic formatting elements (bold headings, bullet points, tables) enhance readability
The inclusion of a word count and title page indicates awareness of IB presentation requirements
weaknesses: Figure and table captions are inconsistently labeled and often unnumbered
In-text citations are sparse, and the bibliography contains broken links and duplicate entries
The table of contents omits page numbers, and page numbering is not clearly applied
Some section labels and numbering appear mistyped or misaligned
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the core structural requirements yet exhibits multiple formatting inconsistencies and incomplete adherence to IB style. The work demonstrates a clearly appropriate structure but falls short of precise formatting standards, warranting a 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Clarity and focus**: The research question is clearly worded and narrow enough to be answerable within 4,000 words. It specifies the firm, two elements of the marketing mix and the performance metric to be judged (revenue growth).

**Partial shortcomings**: The phrase âto what extentâ implies some measurement, yet no time horizon or comparative benchmark (e.g., against competitors or other Ps) is declared, leaving partial ambiguity. In addition, only Product and Promotion are examined even though the introduction occasionally drifts into distribution and pricing, suggesting focus is not always maintained.

**Overall judgment**: The RQ is clear but only partially focused and connected to the ensuing discussion. These characteristics place it in Band 3-4, at the lower end (score 3).

**Argument development**: The essay follows a logical progressionâtools â analysis â conclusionâand attempts to weigh the relative influence of Product versus Promotion. Some evaluation of tool limitations (e.g., BCG oversimplification) shows emergent criticality.

**Critical engagement**: Evaluation is often generic and unbalanced; for instance, the conclusion largely restates earlier points without rigorously challenging data quality or alternative explanations (e.g., macroeconomic factors, competitor actions). Counter-arguments are minimal.

**Band alignment**: The argument is âwell-developedâ but only partially critical, matching the lower end of Band 4-6 (score 4).

**Demonstrated understanding**: The student displays clear grasp of core BM concepts such as the BCG matrix, Ansoff matrix, SWOT, 7Ps and first-mover advantage. Definitions are offered and tools are applied to the firmâs context, revealing coherent knowledge of subject content.

**Breadth vs. depth**: While coverage is broad, explanations sometimes remain descriptive; for example, the treatment of âcash cowsâ versus âstarsâ largely restates textbook definitions without fully tailoring insights to Nvidiaâs nuanced segments. Nonetheless, the knowledge shown is accurate and logically organised.

**Overall**: The clarity and coherence of subject knowledge, albeit with modest depth, justify the upper end of Band 3-4 (score 4).

**Accuracy**: Key marketing and strategic terms (e.g., âmarket penetrationâ, âquestion marksâ, âCACâ) are generally used correctly, demonstrating working familiarity with standard BM vocabulary.

**Consistency & precision issues**: Occasional inaccuracies and imprecise phrasing appear (âeconomics and thoiresâ, confusion between âgraphics cardsâ and âGPUsâ, conflation of product vs. promotion). Referencing vocabulary (e.g., âresearch paper referredâ) is informal, and several grammatical errors detract from precision.

**Judgement**: Terminology is mostly accurate but not consistently precise, fitting Band 3-4 at a mid-level score 3.

**Analytical efforts**: The student applies BCG, Ansoff and SWOT to dissect Nvidiaâs product and promotion strategies, and links revenue trends to AI-driven demand. There is some attempt to interpret line-graph data and connect strategic tools to revenue outcomes.

**Depth constraints**: Analysis often remains surface-level, reiterating textbook explanations rather than probing causal mechanisms (e.g., how specific promotional activities translate into sales). Quantitative data are presented but statistical techniques (growth rates, comparisons with competitors, elasticity) are absent, so conclusions are only partly substantiated.

**Overall**: The strand demonstrates clear but not sophisticated analysis with partial support, meriting Band 4-6 at score 4.

**Use of sources**: Revenue figures, market-share statistics and advertising spend estimates are integrated into the discussion and linked to Product and Promotion decisions. Graphs are constructed and referenced, indicating active use rather than simple citation.

**Weaknesses**: Many assertions about Nvidiaâs strategic intent (e.g., customer relations, first-mover advantage) are unsupported by evidence or reference only a single website. In-text citations are largely absent, so source material cannot always be verified.

**Conclusion**: Application is mostly relevant and appropriate but inconsistently rigorous, placing the strand in Band 3-4 at the lower mark (score 3).

**Range and relevance**: The student relies on secondary sources that include annual reports, market-cap websites, research papers and business textbooks. These are generally appropriate for analysing a publicly listed corporation. Several visual data sets (revenue graphs, BCG diagram, Ansoff grid) demonstrate some informed selection.

**Selection quality**: However, the methodology section is brief and generic; there is no explanation of criteria for choosing sources, nor a discussion of alternative approaches (e.g., primary interviews, competitor data). Limitations are noted but only superficially (âbiasesâ¦ tampered documentsâ).

**Band placement**: The essay therefore shows a âgenerally relevantâ set of methods with some evidence of informed selection, aligning with Band 3-4 (score 3).

**Breadth of research**: The bibliography lists a reasonable variety of secondary sources: annual reports, financial databases, research papers and textbook definitions. Data are up-to-date (2024 figures) and relevant to the RQ.

**Depth limitations**: Source credibility is uneven (e.g., SaveMyExams revision site, MediaRadar advertising spend) and the absence of triangulation or primary research narrows reliability. Some data (e.g., proprietary graphs) lack clear provenance.

**Assessment**: The investigation shows âgoodâ but not excellent research that is clearly relevant to the question, consistent with Band 4-6 at the lower edge (score 4).

**Strengths**: A title page, word count, headings and tables are evident, and visuals are included. Basic formatting conventions (bold headings, bullet points) aid readability.

**Deficiencies**: In-text citations are sparse, some images lack full source attribution, and there is no clear page numbering visible in the extract. Table and figure labeling is inconsistent, and the bibliography contains broken links and duplicated information, indicating only partial adherence to IB style expectations.

**Overall**: With some layout elements missing or applied inaccurately, the work meets Band 1-2 requirements, meriting the higher mark (score 2) within that band.

**Positive aspects**: The report follows a conventional EE structure (title page, contents, sections 1-6, bibliography). Headings and sub-headings guide the reader, and visual aids are embedded near the relevant discussion.

**Shortcomings**: Minor inconsistencies appear (e.g., mis-numbered figures, a stray â4.5 Revenue analysis9â, some overly long paragraphs). Nonetheless, all required structural elements are present and largely logical.

**Evaluation**: The structure is clearly appropriate though not flawless, placing it in Band 3-4 with a mid-level score 3.

annotations.comment: Ansoff matrix is presented but not linked to concrete Nvidia initiatives; discuss specific market-product moves to apply the tool.
The Product Development explanation ends abruptly; ensure each paragraph fully addresses the intended strategy point.
First-mover advantage is asserted without citation; reference a credible source for the definition and link to Nvidiaâs history.
Market development discussion states presence in 32 countries but gives no evidence; cite operational or revenue data by region.
Caption âFig 3â repeats the earlier title; differentiate captions and ensure numbering and titles reflect unique content.
The table embedded with the graph is unnumbered and lacks a descriptive title; number tables separately and caption them.
The âResearch paper referredâ entry omits author and date details; include full reference information to meet IB guidelines.
An advertising campaign link is mixed into the bibliography without distinction; separate non-source items or create an appendix for supplementary materials.
Standalone heading âAdvertisementâ appears as a bibliography item; remove or reposition under a separate heading to avoid confusion.
Holding strategy description outlines generic R&D needs but fails to link to Nvidiaâs actual investment levels; integrate specific figures.
Divesting strategy section is truncated; complete the thought and ensure paragraphs are fully developed.
Promotion strategy section names spend levels but lacks analysis of campaign effectiveness or ROI; include performance metrics.
Title formatting is inconsistent; âmarketing strategy of Nvidiaâ should use title case and capitalize proper nouns to meet IB presentation standards.
Including the word count at the start meets the IB formatting requirement and demonstrates awareness of the 4,000-word limit.
Assuming that high market share equates to high profitability overlooks cost structures; examine profitability drivers explicitly.
The discussion of âQuestion marksâ lacks supporting market-share data and risk assessment; quantify the investment required.
The research question omits a defined time horizon or benchmark, which leaves the scope partially ambiguous and undermines the focus of the investigation.
The entry â4.5 Revenue analysis9â appears mistyped and misnumbered; ensure consistency in section labels for clarity.
Financial figures (market cap, revenue, market share) lack in-text citations; include precise references to support data credibility.
The table of contents lists sections but omits page numbers, making navigation cumbersome and detracting from the reportâs usability.
The methodology section lists source types but does not explain how sources were selected or their relevance; clarify selection criteria.
Limitations of secondary data use are noted superficially; discuss specific biases and data reliability to deepen methodological rigour.
Descriptions of BCG and Ansoff matrices remain generic; apply each tool directly to Nvidiaâs context to demonstrate deeper subject understanding.
Weakness analysis of the Ansoff matrix is descriptive; critically evaluate how omitted external/internal factors specifically impact Nvidiaâs strategy.
SWOT definitions are well-stated but lack tailored examples of Nvidiaâs specific strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats to show applied knowledge.
The SWOT discussion ends mid-sentence and reads as a run-on paragraph; improve structure and ensure complete, clear explanations.
Product portfolio discussion presents revenue trends without citing sources; add references for graph data to validate analysis.
Caption âFig 1â is too terse; format as âFigure 1: â¦â and provide a concise title to improve clarity.
BCG matrix analysis restates tool functions without interrogating why specific products occupy each quadrant; deepen causal evaluation.
The BCG diagram is included but not linked to data metrics; explain the quantitative basis for positioning Nvidiaâs product categories.
Discussion of âStarsâ category repeats textbook definition; critically assess resource implications and potential market shifts for Nvidia.
Critique of the BCG matrix here reiterates earlier points without new insight; avoid repetition and deepen evaluation.
The methodology justification for relying exclusively on secondary data is cursory; explain selection criteria and consider alternatives to strengthen focus.
Cash cow analysis omits specific performance metrics for Nvidiaâs datacenter products; include data to support claims.
Revenue trend interpretation overlooks competitor context; compare Nvidiaâs growth rates with peer firms to deepen analysis.
The conclusion summarises findings but does not explicitly answer the research question to what extent, weakening the reportâs focus.
The graph lacks explicit source attribution and figure caption details; include origin and properly styled label in line with IB presentation.
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted and lack hanging indents; apply a single citation style throughout.
Conclusion paragraphs are repetitive and could be more concise; refine language to improve readability and impact.
Summary in the final paragraph remains descriptive; critically evaluate the interplay of product and promotion in driving growth.
The opening line of the penultimate page is orphaned and disconnected; check pagination and ensure text continuity.
URL-only bibliography entries lack italicized titles or author names; provide full bibliographic details for each source.
The same research-methodology link appears twice with slight variations; remove duplicates and consolidate entries.
The annual report URL breaks mid-line and is not formatted as a hyperlink anchor; ensure long URLs are handled cleanly.
A solitary list number â3.â appears without context; ensure numbered lists are complete and correctly ordered.
CompaniesMarketCap.com is cited twice identically; remove repetition and unify the reference under one entry.
The entry for âNvidia (NVDA) â revenueâ is not italicized or formatted consistently; apply a uniform citation style for web sources.
